rationale: Y correctly recognizes that the major reviewer concern (endpoint restrictions resolved from the wrong/auto-detected pipeline) was already fixed via get_pipeline_config's deploy-override-first ordering and the new regression test, and independently surfaces the trust_remote_code default flip (matching the alex-jw-brooks inline bug comment) plus related caching/error-response territory the human reviewers touched. X's findings (missing try/except, stale docstring, import coupling, kwarg naming) are plausible and concrete but mostly orthogonal to what the ground-truth reviewers actually flagged, giving it much lower recall despite similar precision and actionability. Both omit several ground-truth points (positional-arg risk, debug-msg scope, mutability-of-cached-config specifics) and neither has a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
